title: Sit down, the table is free
summary: In a Bratislava park, an old man at a chess table invites a teenager to play — and offers something more than the game.

body.speaker: Lukas
body.text: Excuse me. I am sorry to look. The pieces are nice.

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: Sit down. The other side is free.

body.speaker: Lukas
body.text: Oh, I cannot play. I am very bad.

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: Everybody is bad. Even I am bad. But I have been bad for sixty years, so I have learned how to be bad nicely. Sit.

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: You take white. White moves first. The first move is the smallest decision in your life today.

body.speaker: Lukas
body.text: What is this place? I walked through the park and I saw many small tables. All chess.

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: This is our chess club. We meet here every day except when it rains. We have no roof. We have no president. We have no fee. We have eight stone tables and we have time.

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: Some days, two. Some days, twenty. In summer, more. In winter, only the old ones. Old men do not feel the cold. We have already lost the warmth a long time ago.

body.speaker: Lukas
body.text: How long has this club been here?

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: Before the wall fell. Before the country changed name. The tables are older than the new flag. Move.

body.speaker: Stefan
body.text: Good. Now I move. Now you think. In chess and in life, the best move is the one you regret least tomorrow.

annotations.meaningVi: một cách dễ thương / nhã (trạng từ)
hơi ấm / sự ấm áp (danh từ)
bức tường sụp đổ (ám chỉ bức tường Berlin)

grammarNotes.title: Superlative with 'least'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Least' là so sánh nhất của 'little', dùng để chỉ mức độ THẤP NHẤT của một tính chất. Trong bài: 'The best move is the one you regret least tomorrow.' (Nước đi tốt nhất là nước mà mai mình hối tiếc ít nhất.) So sánh: 'The cheapest = giá thấp nhất.' 'You regret least = bạn hối tiếc ít nhất.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Least' is the superlative of 'little' and marks the LOWEST level of a quality. In the dialogue: 'The best move is the one you regret least tomorrow.' Compare 'the cheapest' (lowest price) with 'you regret least' (lowest amount of regret).

translationVi:
Lukas: Cháu xin lỗi. Cháu lỡ nhìn. Bộ cờ đẹp quá.
Stefan: Ngồi đi. Bên kia còn trống.
Lukas: Ôi, cháu không biết chơi. Cháu chơi dở lắm.
Stefan: Ai cũng dở. Cả ông cũng dở. Nhưng ông đã dở sáu mươi năm rồi, nên ông biết cách dở một cách dễ thương. Ngồi đi.
Lukas: Vâng. Trắng hay đen ạ?
Stefan: Cháu cầm trắng. Trắng đi trước. Nước đầu là quyết định nhỏ nhất của cháu hôm nay.
Lukas: Chỗ này là gì vậy ông? Cháu đi qua công viên và thấy nhiều bàn nhỏ. Toàn là cờ.
Stefan: Đây là câu lạc bộ cờ của bọn ông. Bọn ông gặp mỗi ngày trừ khi trời mưa. Không mái che. Không chủ tịch. Không phí. Có tám cái bàn đá và có thời gian.
Lukas: Có bao nhiêu người tới?
Stefan: Có hôm hai. Có hôm hai mươi. Hè nhiều hơn. Đông chỉ còn ông già. Ông già không sợ lạnh. Bọn ông đã mất hơi ấm từ lâu rồi.
Lukas: Câu lạc bộ ở đây bao lâu rồi ạ?
Stefan: Trước khi bức tường sụp. Trước khi đất nước đổi tên. Mấy cái bàn còn xưa hơn lá cờ mới. Đi đi.
Lukas: Dạ. Cháu đi quân tốt.
Stefan: Tốt. Giờ ông đi. Giờ cháu suy nghĩ. Trong cờ và trong đời, nước đi tốt nhất là nước mà ngày mai cháu hối tiếc ít nhất.

questions.prompt: Why was Lukas looking at the table?
options: Because the chess pieces are nice
The free seat on the other side, to play
questions.explanation: 'Sit down. The other side is free.'

questions.prompt: What does Stefan say about being bad at chess?
options: Everybody is bad — he has learned to be bad nicely
He is the best player in the park
Nobody should play if they are bad
questions.explanation: His warm reply to Lukas's worry.

questions.prompt: What does Stefan say about the club's fees and structure?
options: No roof, no president, no fee
questions.explanation: 'We have no roof. We have no president. We have no fee.'

questions.prompt: How many stone tables are there?
How does Stefan describe the club's age?
options: Older than the new flag, from before the wall fell
questions.explanation: 'The tables are older than the new flag.'

questions.prompt: What does Stefan say is the best move in chess and life?
options: The one you regret least tomorrow
The one your opponent does not expect
questions.explanation: Direct quote on the last line.

options: A public place with simple rules can teach gentle wisdom to anyone who sits down.
Old men should not play with teenagers.
Chess is too hard for beginners.
questions.explanation: Stefan welcomes Lukas, then offers a small life lesson.

cloze.template:
Lukas: Excuse me. I am sorry to look. The {{b1}} are nice.
Stefan: Sit down. The other side is {{b2}}.
Lukas: Oh, I cannot play. I am very bad.
Stefan: Everybody is bad. Even I am bad. But I have been bad for {{b3}} years, so I have learned how to be bad nicely. Sit.
Lukas: Okay. White or black?
Stefan: You take white. {{b4}} moves first. The first move is the smallest decision in your life today.
Lukas: What is this place? I walked through the park and I saw many small tables. All chess.
Stefan: This is our chess club. We meet here every day except when it rains. We have no roof. We have no president. We have no {{b5}}. We have eight stone tables and we have time.
Lukas: How many people come?
Stefan: Some days, two. Some days, twenty. In summer, more. In winter, only the {{b6}} ones. Old men do not feel the cold. We have already lost the warmth a long time ago.
Lukas: How long has this club been here?
Stefan: Before the {{b7}} fell. Before the country changed name. The tables are older than the new {{b8}}. Move.
Lukas: Sorry. I moved a {{b9}}.
Stefan: Good. Now I move. Now you think. In chess and in life, the best move is the one you regret {{b10}} tomorrow.

blanks.explanation: White always moves first in chess.
Only the old ones in winter.
Before the wall fell — the Berlin Wall era.
Older than the new flag of the new country.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Stefan says, 'The best move is the one you regret least tomorrow.' Is there a small decision you must make this week where that idea — choosing the option of least future regret — would help you more than choosing the 'best' option?
